english: morrow, the day after to-morrow, on the fourth day; γzės-rnyiń (gzhes rnying) Cs. = γzì-niń (gzhi ning).
(= bżugs-pa (bzhugs pa) yet less used), resp. for to sit, stay, wait, cuń tsam γzės śig (cung tsam gzhes shig) wait a little! Dzl. 222, 12 (another reading: bżugs śig (bzhugs shig)).
for bżó-ba (bzho ba), v. ǰó-ba ('jo ba).
v. ǰóg-pa ('jog pa).

the side of the body, = glo (glo); γżogs γyas γyon (gzhogs g.yas g.yon) the right and left side Sch.; γżógs - su (gzhogs su) sideways Sch.; γżogs slóń-ba (gzhogs slong ba) Lex., γżogs-sloń byė́d-pa (gzhogs slong byed pa) Cs. to speak allusively; γżogs-smód byė́d-pa (gzhogs smod byed pa) to prejudice a person against another insidiously, to create enmity Thgy.; it is also used like a verb: γżógs-te rtsáb-pa (gzhogs te rtsab pa) to be insolent with a fair appearance, opp. to ṅor (ngor) downright Thgy. - γżogs-p'yė́d ná-ba (gzhogs phyed na ba) Do. prob. an inaccurate expression for pain in one side.

wooden basin, trough, tub, washing-tub; k'yi-γżoń (khyi gzhong) (col. *k'yib-żoń* (khyib zhong)) trough for feeding dogs and other animals, also manger W.; *t'ud-żoń* (thud zhong) W. prob. id.; *čag-żoń* (chag zhong) W. trough for dry horse-meat; *t'ab-żoń* (thab zhong) winnowing-tray, inst. of a shovel; in books the word is used in a wider sense, in such expressions as γser- (gser-), dṅul- (dngul-), k'ár- (mkhar-), rdo-γzoń (rdo gzhong).
Lex. = lǰońs (ljongs).

1. sbst. v. bżón-pa (bzhon pa). - 2. adj. young, γżón-pa de na-ré (gzhon pa de na re) the younger one said Mil.; rgyál-po sku-ná γżón-pa (rgyal po sku na gzhon pa) the young king; bdag γżón-pas (bdag gzhon pas) as I am still young, I as the younger one, the youngest Dzl.; γżón-pa gá-żig (gzhon pa ga zhig) some young people Mil.; γżón-dus bu-mė́d (gzhon dus bu med) who in their younger years had no children; γżón-nu (gzhon nu) a youth, frq., γżón-nu-ts'o (gzhon nu tsho) plur. Mil.; γżón-nu-ma (gzhon nu ma) or bu-mo γżón-nu (bu mo gzhon nu) Dzl. virgin, maiden, girl; sé-ba γżón-nu (se ba gzhon nu) a young rose Wdn.; γżón-nu dań brál-bar byė́d-pa (gzhon nu dang bral bar byed pa) to deprive a girl of her virginity Cs., γżon-nu-brál (gzhon nu bral) a girl that has lost her virginity Cs.; γżón-nu-nas (gzhon nu nas) from a child, from infancy Mil.; γżon-grógs (gzhon grogs) youthful companion Mil.; γżón-śa-can (gzhon sha can) with youthful flesh, γżón-śa-can-du gyúr-ba (gzhon sha can du gyur ba) Glr. to grow young again.

1. me-γżób (me gzhob) singeing, or what has been singed, wool, hair, feathers etc.; a mark from burning; γżób-dri (gzhob dri) Sch. also γżob-ró (gzhob ro) smell of singeing; γżób-tu gyúr-ba (gzhob tu gyur ba) to be singed, seared Pth.; *żob gyáb-pa* (zhob rgyab pa) C. to singe off; fig. ṅai lus-sė́ms γżób-tu t'al (ngai lus sems gzhob tu thal) Glr. my body and soul were seared, deeply afflicted. - 2. W. a crash, e.g. of a tree breaking down.
1. v. ǰoms-pa ('joms pa). - 2. to break in two, to tear Sch.; in W. used of metal vessels bent or bruised.
v. ǰor ('jor).

1. to apply one's self diligently Cs., čös-la t'ugs γżól-ba (chos la thugs gzhol ba) Pth. id. resp. - 2. to comprehend, to fathom (?) Sch. - 3. resp. for báb-pa ('bab pa) to alight, light from, dismount, v. c'ibs (chibs); cf. also żol (zhol).
for bżos (bzhos), v. ǰó-ba ('jo ba).
in Lexx. mentioned as the same with brlán-pa (brlan pa).

1. large intestine, = γnyė́-ma (gnye ma); bżag-sgór-mo (bzhag sgor mo) the windings of the intestines Glr., Mil. - 2. certain muscles under the arms Mng. - 3. Sch.: 'flesh of animals that died of disease'.
1. v. ˳ǰóg-pa ('jog pa). - 2. to tear, wear, intrs., of cloth etc.; to burst, crack, split C., W.
also bżád-pa (bzhad pa) Pth. swan; bżad-dkár (bzhad dkar) Lex.; bżad-ldán (bzhad ldan) Schr.: 'a pond with swans on it'.

to laugh, smile Glr.; bżád-k'a-ma (bzhad kha ma) a girl with a smiling face Mil.; bżad-gád (bzhad gad) laughter, t'ė́g-pa (theg pa) to raise (a laughter) Mil., bżad-gád-mk'an (bzhad gad mkhan) Tar. buffoon, jester; bżád-mo (bzhad mo) smile, laughing, laughter, bżád-mo bżád-pa (bzhad mo bzhad pa) to laugh; bżád-pa-mo (bzhad pa mo), bżad-ldán-ma (bzhad ldan ma) n. of a goddess, Ssk. Hasawati Cs.
v. ǰáb-pa ('jab pa).
1. also γżáms-pa (gzhams pa) Schr.? to stroke, p'yág-gis (phyag gis) resp. with the hand, to coax, caress; hence bżáms-te (bzhams te) Dzl.
